---
title: "Synthetic Control Report Draft"
---

## Purpose

This is a skeleton draft for a longer report on synthetic control design and implementation.

## Intended Scope

- when synthetic control is a good fit for an evaluation problem
- how donor-pool choice shapes credibility
- how pre-treatment fit, placebo checks, and sensitivity analysis should be reported

## Planned Structure

### 1. Policy Question And Case Selection

Draft notes:

- clarify the treated unit
- define intervention timing
- specify the target outcome path

### 2. Identification Logic

Draft notes:

- why synthetic control is a weighted comparative-case design
- the role of pre-treatment fit
- limits when the treated case is not well supported by the donor pool

### 3. Design Inputs

Draft notes:

- donor-pool selection
- predictor selection
- pre-treatment window choice

### 4. Diagnostics

Draft notes:

- pre-treatment fit metrics
- placebo and permutation checks
- leave-one-out and donor-pool sensitivity

### 5. Worked Example

Draft notes:

- connect to the synthetic-control notes and design labs

### 6. Reporting Checklist

Draft notes:

- what must be shown before a synthetic-control estimate is decision-ready

## Related Material

- [Synthetic Control](../notes/synthetic-control.qmd)
- [Synthetic Control Design Lab](../labs/synthetic-control-design-lab.qmd)
- [Synthetic Control Donor Pool Lab](../labs/synthetic-control-donor-pool-lab.qmd)
